—This chapter is the same as [reference]. See what is said about it there. It is very noticeable that what is in Samuel said to be “for your word's sake” is here said to be, “for your servant’s sake,” verse [number]. Jesus Christ is both the Word of God and the Servant of God; and it is for his sake, because of his mediation, that the promises are made good to all believers; it is in him that they are yes and amen. It is done for His sake, it is made known for his sake; we owe all this greatness to him, from him we are to expect all these great things. They are the unsearchable riches of Christ, which, if by faith we see in themselves and see in the Lord Jesus, we cannot but magnify as the only true greatness, and speak honorably of them. For this blessedness may we look amidst the trials of life, and when we feel the hand of death upon us; and seek it for our children after us.
